plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and lower overall market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and exit positions below stop.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and stability.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single stock concentration.
Initiate a small 5% position in TSLA, a high-quality scanner setup.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on stability and lower drawdown. Risk is reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and diversifying, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and exit positions below stop.
Slightly increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income exposure.
Add to CRWD, a strong scanner setup, increasing quality tech exposure to 15%.
Initiate a new 8% position in TSLA, a top scanner setup.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer by carefully managing overall exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a risk-first growth approach, blending broad index, quality tech, and careful leverage. It aims for moderate growth with controlled drawdown, maintaining a cash buffer.

moves.note: Add top scanner setup, high conviction

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate positions below stop and reduce overall risk.
Increase SMH to 18% to lean into the semiconductor sector's uptrend.
Add to CRWD, a high-scoring AI/Software name, increasing to 18%.
Initiate a 10% position in TSLA, a top scanner setup for aggressive growth.
Buy a 7% position in NVDU, a high R-value scanner setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into high-scoring setups and using leverage, accepting higher risk. Expect increased volatility and larger drawdowns; leveraged ETFs also face daily decay and rebalancing risk.

plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and lower overall market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and exit positions below stop.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income ETF exposure.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented broad index exposure.
Sell all CRWD to exit high-beta single name exposure.
Maintain a significant cash buffer, prioritizing low drawdown and steady distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. It aims for low drawdown by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad index, but distributions are variable and NAV can still decline.
